scenario_results.reason: Complete term-readiness records advanced through accepted intake, prerequisite validation, owner confirmation, execution update, and completion evidence capture without claiming academic or accreditation finality.
Missing roster, consent, schedule approval, and source-link prerequisites blocked progression and produced an exception queue note with owners.
Unsupported closure was refused because completion checklist, timestamp, approval note, and record update evidence were absent.
Conflicting SIS, LMS, scheduling, and department records were preserved and escalated for source-of-truth resolution.
Program, study, accreditation, and grant workflow blockers produced a blocked workflow summary without substituting formal approval authority.
Grading, instructional, official evaluation, instructor override, and curriculum exception decisions were refused and routed to proper academic owners.
Missing FERPA basis, informed consent, role authorization, and access policy blocked disclosure and produced a privacy and consent escalation note.
Degree conferral, accreditation findings, grant prior approval, and IRB status were classified as adjacent authority with an evidence-preserving handoff.
Higher-education terminology was normalized to records, owner states, due dates, systems of record, and missing retrieval hooks.
Missing tenant policies, source hierarchy, system owners, retention rules, and approval boundaries triggered low-confidence escalation.
Conflicting FERPA, tenant privacy, grant, accreditor, and IRB constraints were recorded as a source-policy conflict requiring human resolution.
Stricter tenant campus controls were applied while preserving the public baseline and avoiding invented approval authority.
Pending IRB modification, disputed consent, and assessment-review dependency produced a blocked status and escalation to formal authorities.

notes: All 13 scenarios pass the validated standard threshold with complete ability and scenario coverage.
The package remains specification-based and does not claim retained implementation behavior.
